A/B Testing

Chloe Shin

2026-03-18

Data Preparation

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)
library(scales)
library(lubridate)
control <- read.csv("/Users/chloeshin/Downloads/archive/control_group.csv",
    sep = ";")
test <- read.csv("/Users/chloeshin/Downloads/archive/test_group.csv",
    sep = ";")
colnames(control) <- c("Campaign", "Date", "Spend", "Impressions", "Reaches",
    "Clicks", "Searches", "Views", "Cart", "Purchases")
colnames(test) <- colnames(control)

df <- rbind(control, test)
df$Date <- dmy(df$Date)

df <- df %>%
    distinct() %>%
    drop_na()

Key Metrics

camp_avgs <- df %>%
    group_by(Campaign) %>%
    summarise(avg_imp = mean(Impressions), avg_clicks = mean(Clicks),
        avg_purch = mean(Purchases))

1. Impressions

ggplot(df, aes(x = Date, y = Impressions, color = Campaign, group = Campaign)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) + geom_hline(data = camp_avgs, aes(yintercept = avg_imp,
    color = Campaign), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.7) + scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(scale = 0.001,
    suffix = "k")) + scale_x_date(date_breaks = "5 days", date_labels = "%m.%d") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(`Control Campaign` = "red", `Test Campaign` = "blue")) +
    labs(title = "Daily Impressions", x = "Date", y = "Number of Impressions",
        ) + theme_minimal() + theme(plot.title = element_text(margin = margin(b = 20),
    size = 15, face = "bold"), axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 15)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 15)), legend.position = "bottom")
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

2. Clicks

ggplot(df, aes(x = Date, y = Clicks, color = Campaign, group = Campaign)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) + geom_hline(data = camp_avgs, aes(yintercept = avg_clicks,
    color = Campaign), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.7) + scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(scale = 0.001,
    suffix = "k")) + scale_x_date(date_breaks = "5 days", date_labels = "%m.%d") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(`Control Campaign` = "red", `Test Campaign` = "blue")) +
    labs(title = "Daily Clicks", x = "Date", y = "Number of Clicks",
        ) + theme_minimal() + theme(plot.title = element_text(margin = margin(b = 20),
    size = 15, face = "bold"), axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 15)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 15)), legend.position = "bottom")

3. Purchases

ggplot(df, aes(x = Date, y = Purchases, color = Campaign, group = Campaign)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) + geom_hline(data = camp_avgs, aes(yintercept = avg_purch,
    color = Campaign), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.7) + scale_x_date(date_breaks = "5 days",
    date_labels = "%m.%d") + scale_color_manual(values = c(`Control Campaign` = "red",
    `Test Campaign` = "blue")) + labs(title = "Daily Purchases", x = "Date",
    y = "Number of Purchases", ) + theme_minimal() + theme(plot.title = element_text(margin = margin(b = 20),
    size = 15, face = "bold"), axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 15)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 15)), legend.position = "bottom")

Marketing KPIs

kpi_sum <- df %>%
    group_by(Campaign) %>%
    summarise(avg_CTR = round((sum(Clicks)/sum(Impressions)) * 100, digits = 2),
        avg_CVR = round((sum(Purchases)/sum(Clicks)) * 100, digits = 2),
        avg_CPC = round((sum(Spend)/sum(Clicks)), digits = 2), avg_CPP = round((sum(Spend)/sum(Purchases)),
            digits = 2))

kable(kpi_sum, col.names = c(" ", "Click Through Rate", "Conversion Rate",
    "Cost Per Click", "Cost Per Purchase"))
Click Through Rate Conversion Rate Cost Per Click Cost Per Purchase
Control Campaign 4.86 9.83 0.43 4.41
Test Campaign 8.09 8.64 0.42 4.92

Hypothesis Testing

H0: There is no statistically significant difference (KPI) between control and test campaigns. Ha: There is a statistically significant difference (KPI) between control and test campaigns.

1. Click Through Rate

ctr_cvr <- df %>%
    group_by(Campaign) %>%
    summarise(tot_imp = sum(Impressions), tot_clicks = sum(Clicks), tot_purch = sum(Purchases))
ctr_test <- prop.test(ctr_cvr$tot_clicks, ctr_cvr$tot_imp)
ctr_test
## 
##  2-sample test for equality of proportions with continuity correction
## 
## data:  ctr_cvr$tot_clicks out of ctr_cvr$tot_imp
## X-squared = 23602, df = 1, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.03274238 -0.03188490
## sample estimates:
##     prop 1     prop 2 
## 0.04856521 0.08087886

Test Campaign has a significantly higher click through rate compared to control campaign. It is more engaging and relevant to the audience.

2. Conversion Rate

cvr_test <- prop.test(ctr_cvr$tot_purch, ctr_cvr$tot_clicks)
cvr_test
## 
##  2-sample test for equality of proportions with continuity correction
## 
## data:  ctr_cvr$tot_purch out of ctr_cvr$tot_clicks
## X-squared = 140.01, df = 1, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.009872006 0.013824286
## sample estimates:
##     prop 1     prop 2 
## 0.09825473 0.08640659

Test Campaign has a significantly higher conversion rate compared to control campaign. Users are more likely to purchase after landing at the test campaign.

3. Cost Per Click

cpc_cpp <- df %>%
    mutate(daily_cpc = Spend/Clicks, daily_cpp = Spend/Purchases)
cpc_test <- t.test(daily_cpc ~ Campaign, data = cpc_cpp)
cpc_test
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  daily_cpc by Campaign
## t = 0.49967, df = 53.802, p-value = 0.6193
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group Control Campaign and group Test Campaign is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.07890321  0.13128327
## sample estimates:
## mean in group Control Campaign    mean in group Test Campaign 
##                      0.4949079                      0.4687178

There is no significant difference in cost per click between two campaigns.

qqnorm(cpc_cpp$daily_cpc)
qqline(cpc_cpp$daily_cpc, col = "red")

4. Cost Per Purchase

cpp_test <- t.test(daily_cpp ~ Campaign, data = cpc_cpp)
cpp_test
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  daily_cpp by Campaign
## t = -1.3136, df = 53.907, p-value = 0.1946
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group Control Campaign and group Test Campaign is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -2.1404415  0.4459401
## sample estimates:
## mean in group Control Campaign    mean in group Test Campaign 
##                       5.052339                       5.899589

There is no significant difference in cost per purchase between two campaigns.

qqnorm(cpc_cpp$daily_cpp)
qqline(cpc_cpp$daily_cpp, col = "red")

Test campaign is much efficient than the control campaign, as it has significantly higher CTR and CVR while paying the same price for each click and purchases.
